findings.message: The recursive walkRouter implementation is clean and effectively handles the nested structure of tRPC v11 routers, including plain object namespaces.
The 4-priority detection chain (explicit -> auto-crud -> explicit subject -> pending) provides a robust auto-discovery mechanism with clear override paths for developers.
Initialization timing in TrpcModule.onModuleInit is correct. It runs after fastify registration and after PluginModule's initial scan, ensuring all core and initial plugin routes are captured.
Rebuild logic in router.ts correctly handles dynamic plugin loading/unloading by updating the registry only if it has already been initialized.
Subscription procedures are automatically mapped to 'read' actions via the query fallback. This is a safe and reasonable default for auto-discovery.
The singularize helper correctly handles common project table patterns including 'ies' (currencies) and 's' (users, roles), while safely bypassing 'ss' (glass) and 'us' (status).

summary: Phase 6A implementation is highly maintainable and correctly integrated. The path-driven approach aligns perfectly with the project's infrastructure policy refactor. No critical issues found.
